commentaries.sankara_english: 1.27 Sri Sankaracharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.10.
commentaries.ramanuja_english: 1.26 - 1.47 Arjuna said --- Sañjaya said -- Sañjaya continued: The high-minded Arjuna, extremely kind, deeply friendly, and supremely righteous, having brothers like himself, though repeatedly deceived by the treacherous attempts of your people like burning in the lac-house etc., and therefore fit to be killed by him with the help of the Supreme Person, nevertheless said, 'I will not fight.' He felt weak, overcome as he was by his love and extreme compassion for his relatives. He was also filled with fear, not knowing what was righteous and what unrighteous. His mind was tortured by grief, because of the thought of future separation from his relations. So he threw away his bow and arrow and sat on the chariot as if to fast to death.

verses.devanagari:
अर्जुन उवाच
कृपया परयाविष्टो विषीदन्निदमब्रावीत्।
दृष्ट्वेमं स्वजनं कृष्ण युयुत्सुं समुपस्थितम् ॥1.28॥
verses.transliteration:
1.28 kṛpayā parayāviṣṭo viṣīdann idam abravīt 
dṛṣṭvemān svajanān kṛṣṇa yuyutsūn samavasthitān

translations.adidevananda: 1.28 He was filled with deep compassion and said these words in despair ... Arjuna said: ...O Kṛṣṇa, when I look on these, my kinsmen present here, eager for battle,
translations.gambhirananda: 1.28 Arjuna said -- O Kṛṣṇa, seeing these relatives and friends who have assembled here with the intention of fighting, my limbs become languid and my mouth becomes completely dry.
translations.zaehner: (28) was filled with deep compassion and, desponding, spoke these [words]: 'Kṛṣṇa, when I see these mine own folk standing [before me], spoiling for the light,
translations.sivananda: 1.28. Arjuna said -- Seeing these, my kinsmen, O Kṛṣṇa, arrayed, eager to fight.
translations.gemini:
Arjuna said: 
 Overcome by supreme compassion, 
 Despairing, he spoke this: 
 Having seen these my own people, O Dark one (Kṛṣṇa), 
 Approaching, desiring to fight,
translations.stoller_miller:
Dejected, filled with strange pity,
he said this:
“Krishna, I see my kinsmen
gathered here, wanting war.

commentaries.ramanuja_sanskrit: ॥ 1.28 ॥ अर्जुन उवाच -- संजय उवाच -- स तु पार्थो महामनाः परमकारुणिको दीर्घबन्धुः परमधार्मिकः सभ्रातृको भवद्भिः अतिघोरैः मारणैः जतुगृहादिभिः असकृद् वञ्चितः अपि परमपुरुषसहायः अपि हनिष्यमाणान् भवदीयान् विलोक्य बन्धुस्नेहेन परमया च कृपया धर्माधर्मभयेन च अतिमात्रस्विन्नसर्वगात्रः सर्वथा अहं न योत्स्यामि इति उक्त्वा बन्धुविश्लेषजनितशोकसंविग्नमानसः सशरं चापं विसृज्य रथोपस्थे उपाविशत् ।
commentaries.madhva_sanskrit: ॥ 1.28 ॥ Sri Madhvacharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.11.
commentaries.madhusudana_sanskrit: ॥ 1.28 ॥ तदेव भगवन्तं प्रत्यर्जुनवाक्यमवतारयति संजयः-अर्जुन उवाचेत्यादिना'एवमुक्त्वार्जुनः संख्ये' इत्यतः प्राक्तनेन ग्रन्थेन। तत्र स्वधर्मप्रवृक्तिकारणीभूततत्त्वज्ञानप्रतिबन्धकः स्वपरदेहे आत्मात्मीयाभिभानवतोऽनात्मविदोऽर्जुनस्य युद्धेन स्वपरदेहविनाशप्रसङ्गदर्शिनः शोको महानासीदति तल्लिङ्गकथनेन दर्शयति त्रिभिः श्लोकैः। इमं स्वजनमात्मीयं बन्धुवर्गं युद्धेच्छुं युद्धभूमौ चोपस्थितं दृष्ट्वा स्थितस्य मम। पश्यतो ममेत्यर्थः। अङ्गानि व्यथन्ते मुखं च परिशुष्यतीति श्रमादिनिमित्तशोकापेक्षयातिशयकथनाय सर्वतोभाववाचिपरिशब्दप्रयोगः।
commentaries.sankara_english: 1.28 Sri Sankaracharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.10.
commentaries.ramanuja_english: 1.26 - 1.47 Arjuna said --- Sañjaya said -- Sañjaya continued: The high-minded Arjuna, extremely kind, deeply friendly, and supremely righteous, having brothers like himself, though repeatedly deceived by the treacherous attempts of your people like burning in the lac-house etc., and therefore fit to be killed by him with the help of the Supreme Person, nevertheless said, 'I will not fight.' He felt weak, overcome as he was by his love and extreme compassion for his relatives. He was also filled with fear, not knowing what was righteous and what unrighteous. His mind was tortured by grief, because of the thought of future separation from his relations. So he threw away his bow and arrow and sat on the chariot as if to fast to death.

verses.devanagari:
सीदन्ति मम गात्राणि मुखं च परिशुष्यति।
वेपथुश्च शरीरे मे रोमहर्षश्च जायते ॥1.29॥
verses.transliteration:
1.29 sīdanti mama gātrāṇi mukhaṃ ca pariśuṣyati 
vepathuś ca śarīre me romaharṣaś ca jāyate

translations.adidevananda: 1.29 My limbs are weakened, my mouth gets parched, my body trembles and my hairs stand erect.
translations.gambhirananda: 1.29 And there is trembling in my body, and there is horripilation; the Gāṇḍīva (bow) slips from the hand and even the skin burns intensely.
translations.zaehner: (29) my limbs give way, my mouth dries up, trembling seizes upon my body, and my [body's] hairs stand up in dread.
translations.sivananda: 1.29. My limbs fail and my mouth is parched, my body quivers and my hair stands on end.
translations.gemini:
My limbs are sinking, 
 And my mouth is drying up, 
 And trembling occurs in my body, 
 And my hair stands on end.
translations.stoller_miller:
My limbs sink,
my mouth is parched,
my body trembles,
the hair bristles on my flesh.

commentaries.ramanuja_sanskrit: ॥ 1.29 ॥ अर्जुन उवाच -- संजय उवाच -- स तु पार्थो महामनाः परमकारुणिको दीर्घबन्धुः परमधार्मिकः सभ्रातृको भवद्भिः अतिघोरैः मारणैः जतुगृहादिभिः असकृद् वञ्चितः अपि परमपुरुषसहायः अपि हनिष्यमाणान् भवदीयान् विलोक्य बन्धुस्नेहेन परमया च कृपया धर्माधर्मभयेन च अतिमात्रस्विन्नसर्वगात्रः सर्वथा अहं न योत्स्यामि इति उक्त्वा बन्धुविश्लेषजनितशोकसंविग्नमानसः सशरं चापं विसृज्य रथोपस्थे उपाविशत् ।
commentaries.madhva_sanskrit: ॥ 1.29 ॥ Sri Madhvacharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.11.
commentaries.madhusudana_sanskrit: ॥ 1.29 ॥ वेपथुः कम्पः। रोमहर्षः पुलकितत्वम्। गाण्डीवभ्रंशेनाधैर्यलक्षणं दौर्बल्यम्। त्वक्परिदाहेन चान्तःसन्तापो दर्शितः।
commentaries.sankara_english: 1.29 Sri Sankaracharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.10.
commentaries.ramanuja_english: 1.26 - 1.47 Arjuna said --- Sañjaya said -- Sañjaya continued: The high-minded Arjuna, extremely kind, deeply friendly, and supremely righteous, having brothers like himself, though repeatedly deceived by the treacherous attempts of your people like burning in the lac-house etc., and therefore fit to be killed by him with the help of the Supreme Person, nevertheless said, 'I will not fight.' He felt weak, overcome as he was by his love and extreme compassion for his relatives. He was also filled with fear, not knowing what was righteous and what unrighteous. His mind was tortured by grief, because of the thought of future separation from his relations. So he threw away his bow and arrow and sat on the chariot as if to fast to death.

verses.devanagari:
गाण्डीवं रुांसते हस्तात्त्वक्चैव परिदह्रते।
न च शक्नोम्यवस्थातुं भ्रमतीव च मे मनः ॥1.30॥
verses.transliteration:
1.30 gāṇḍīvaṃ sraṃsate hastāt tvak caiva paridahyate 
na ca śaknomy avasthātuṃ bhramatīva ca me manaḥ

translations.adidevananda: 1.30 The bow Gāṇḍīva slips from my hand and my skin is burning. I can stand no longer. My mind seems to reel.
translations.gambhirananda: 1.30 Moreover, O Keśava (Kṛṣṇa), I am not able to stand firmly, and my mind seems to be whirling. And I notice the omens to be adverse.
translations.zaehner: (30) [My bow,] Gāṇḍīva, slips from my hand, my very skin is all ablaze; I cannot stand and my mind seems to wander.
translations.sivananda: 1.30. The (bow) Gāṇḍīva slips from my hand, and also my skins burns all over; I am unable even to stand and my mind is reeling, as it were.
translations.gemini:
The Gandiva bow slips from my hand, 
 And my skin is burning all over; 
 I am not able to remain standing, 
 And my mind seems to be wandering.
translations.stoller_miller:
The magic bow slips
from my hand, my skin burns,
I cannot stand still,
my mind reels.

commentaries.ramanuja_sanskrit: ॥ 1.30 ॥ अर्जुन उवाच -- संजय उवाच -- स तु पार्थो महामनाः परमकारुणिको दीर्घबन्धुः परमधार्मिकः सभ्रातृको भवद्भिः अतिघोरैः मारणैः जतुगृहादिभिः असकृद् वञ्चितः अपि परमपुरुषसहायः अपि हनिष्यमाणान् भवदीयान् विलोक्य बन्धुस्नेहेन परमया च कृपया धर्माधर्मभयेन च अतिमात्रस्विन्नसर्वगात्रः सर्वथा अहं न योत्स्यामि इति उक्त्वा बन्धुविश्लेषजनितशोकसंविग्नमानसः सशरं चापं विसृज्य रथोपस्थे उपाविशत् ।
commentaries.madhva_sanskrit: ॥ 1.30 ॥ Sri Madhvacharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.11.
commentaries.madhusudana_sanskrit: ॥ 1.30 ॥ अवस्थातुं शरीरं धारयितुं च न शक्रोमीत्यनेन मूच्र्छा सूच्यते। तत्र हेतुः मम मनो भ्रमतीवेति भ्रमणकर्तृसादृश्यं नाम मनसः कश्चिद्विकारविशेषो मूच्र्छायाः पूर्वावस्था। चौ हेतौ। यतवमतो नावस्थातुं शक्नोमीत्यर्थः। पुनरप्यवस्थानासामथ्र्ये कारणमाह -- निमित्तानि च सूचकतया आसन्नदुःखस्य विपरीतानि वामनेत्रस्फुरणादीनि पश्याम्यनुभवामि। अतोऽपि नावस्थातुं शक्नोमीत्यर्थः। अहमनात्मवित्त्वेन दुःखित्वाच्छोकनिबन्धनं क्लेशमनुभवामि, त्वंतु सदानन्दरूपत्वाच्छोकासंसर्गीति कृष्णपदेन सूचितम्। अतः स्वजनदर्शने तुल्येऽपि शोकासंसर्गित्वलक्षणाद्विशेषात्त्वं मामशोकं कुर्विति भावः। केशवपदेन च तत्करणसामथ्र्यम्। को ब्राहृा सृष्टिकर्ता, ईशो रूद्रः संहर्ता, तौ वात्यनुकम्प्यतया गच्छतीति तद्व्युत्पत्तेः। भक्तदुःखकर्षित्वं वा कृष्णापदेनोक्तम् ॥
commentaries.sankara_english: 1.30 Sri Sankaracharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.10.
commentaries.ramanuja_english: 1.26 - 1.47 Arjuna said --- Sañjaya said -- Sañjaya continued: The high-minded Arjuna, extremely kind, deeply friendly, and supremely righteous, having brothers like himself, though repeatedly deceived by the treacherous attempts of your people like burning in the lac-house etc., and therefore fit to be killed by him with the help of the Supreme Person, nevertheless said, 'I will not fight.' He felt weak, overcome as he was by his love and extreme compassion for his relatives. He was also filled with fear, not knowing what was righteous and what unrighteous. His mind was tortured by grief, because of the thought of future separation from his relations. So he threw away his bow and arrow and sat on the chariot as if to fast to death.

verses.devanagari:
निमित्तानि च पश्यामि विपरीतानि केशव।
न च श्रेयोऽनुपश्यामि हत्वा स्वजनमाहवे ॥1.31॥
verses.transliteration:
1.31 nimittāni ca paśyāmi viparītāni keśava 
na ca śreyo ‘nupaśyāmi hatvā svajanam āhave

translations.adidevananda: 1.31 I see, Kṛṣṇa, inauspicious omens. I foresee no good in killing my kinsmen in the fight.
translations.gambhirananda: 1.31 Besides, I do not see any good (to be derived) from killing my own people in battle. O Kṛṣṇa, I do not hanker after victory, nor even a kingdom nor pleasures.
translations.zaehner: (31) Kṛṣṇa, adverse omens too I see, nor can I discern aught good in striking down in battle mine own folk,
translations.sivananda: 1.31. And I see adverse omens, O Keśava. I do not see any good in killing my kinsmen in battle.
translations.gemini:
And I see omens, 
 That are adverse, O Beautiful-haired one (Keśava); 
 And I do not foresee any good, 
 Having slain my own people in battle.
translations.stoller_miller:
I see omens of chaos,
Krishna; I see no good
in killing my kinsmen
in battle.

commentaries.ramanuja_sanskrit: ॥ 1.31 ॥ अर्जुन उवाच -- संजय उवाच -- स तु पार्थो महामनाः परमकारुणिको दीर्घबन्धुः परमधार्मिकः सभ्रातृको भवद्भिः अतिघोरैः मारणैः जतुगृहादिभिः असकृद् वञ्चितः अपि परमपुरुषसहायः अपि हनिष्यमाणान् भवदीयान् विलोक्य बन्धुस्नेहेन परमया च कृपया धर्माधर्मभयेन च अतिमात्रस्विन्नसर्वगात्रः सर्वथा अहं न योत्स्यामि इति उक्त्वा बन्धुविश्लेषजनितशोकसंविग्नमानसः सशरं चापं विसृज्य रथोपस्थे उपाविशत् ।
commentaries.madhva_sanskrit: ॥ 1.31 ॥ Sri Madhvacharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.11.
commentaries.madhusudana_sanskrit: ॥ 1.31 ॥ केशवपदेन च केश्यादिदुष्टदैत्यनिबर्हणेन सर्वदा भक्तान्पालयसीत्यतो मामपि शोकनिवारणेन पालयिष्यसीति सूचितम्। ंव लिङ्गद्वारेण समीचीनप्रवृत्तिहेतुभूतत्त्वज्ञानप्रतिबन्धकीभूतं शोकमुक्त्वा संप्रति तत्कारितां विपरीतप्रवृत्तिहेतुभूतां विपरीतबुदिं्ध दर्शयति -- श्रेयः पुरूषार्थं दृष्टमदृष्टं वा बहुविचारणादनु पश्चादपि न पश्यामि। अस्वजनमपि युद्धे हत्वा श्रेयो न पश्यामि।'द्वाविमौ पुरूषौ लोके सूर्यमण्डलमेदिनौ। परिद्वμककड€¬ोगयुक्तश्च रणे चाभिमुखो हतः ॥ ' इत्यादिना हतस्यैव श्रेयोविशेषाभिधानाद्धन्तुस्तु न किंचित्सुकृतम्। वमस्वजनवधेऽपि श्रेयसोऽभावे स्वजनवधे सुतरां तदभाव इति ज्ञापयितुं स्वजनमित्युक्तम्। वमनाहववधे श्रेयो नास्तीति सिद्धसाधनवारणायाहव इत्युक्तम्।
commentaries.sankara_english: 1.31 Sri Sankaracharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.10.
commentaries.ramanuja_english: 1.26 - 1.47 Arjuna said --- Sañjaya said -- Sañjaya continued: The high-minded Arjuna, extremely kind, deeply friendly, and supremely righteous, having brothers like himself, though repeatedly deceived by the treacherous attempts of your people like burning in the lac-house etc., and therefore fit to be killed by him with the help of the Supreme Person, nevertheless said, 'I will not fight.' He felt weak, overcome as he was by his love and extreme compassion for his relatives. He was also filled with fear, not knowing what was righteous and what unrighteous. His mind was tortured by grief, because of the thought of future separation from his relations. So he threw away his bow and arrow and sat on the chariot as if to fast to death.

verses.devanagari:
न काङ्क्षे विजयं कृष्ण न च राज्यं सुखानि च।
किं नो राज्येन गोविन्द किं भोगैर्जीवितेन वा ॥1.32॥
verses.transliteration:
1.32 na kāṅkṣe vijayaṃ kṛṣṇa na ca rājyaṃ sukhāni ca 
kiṃ no rājyena govinda kiṃ bhogair jīvitena vā

translations.adidevananda: 1.32 I desire no victory, nor empire, nor pleasures. What have we to do with empire, O Kṛṣṇa, or enjoyment or even life?
translations.gambhirananda: 1.32 -- 1.34 O Govinda! What need do we have of a kingdom, or what (need) of enjoyments and livelihood? Those for whom kingdom, enjoyments and pleasures are desired by us, viz teachers, uncles, fathers-in-law, grandsons, brothers-in-law as also relatives-those very ones stand arrayed for battle risking their lives and wealth.
translations.zaehner: (32) Kṛṣṇa, I do not long for victory nor for the kingdom nor yet for things of pleasure. What should I do with a kingdom? What with enjoyments or [even] with life?
translations.sivananda: 1.32. I desire not victory, O Kṛṣṇa, nor kingdom, nor pleasures. Of what avail is dominion to us, O Kṛṣṇa, or pleasures or even life?
translations.gemini:
I do not desire victory, O Dark one (Kṛṣṇa), 
 Nor a kingdom, nor pleasures. 
 What is a kingdom to us, O Finder of cows (Govinda), 
 What are enjoyments, or even life?
translations.stoller_miller:
Krishna, I seek no victory,
or kingship or pleasures.
What use to us are kingship,
delights, or life itself?

commentaries.ramanuja_sanskrit: ॥ 1.32 ॥ अर्जुन उवाच -- संजय उवाच -- स तु पार्थो महामनाः परमकारुणिको दीर्घबन्धुः परमधार्मिकः सभ्रातृको भवद्भिः अतिघोरैः मारणैः जतुगृहादिभिः असकृद् वञ्चितः अपि परमपुरुषसहायः अपि हनिष्यमाणान् भवदीयान् विलोक्य बन्धुस्नेहेन परमया च कृपया धर्माधर्मभयेन च अतिमात्रस्विन्नसर्वगात्रः सर्वथा अहं न योत्स्यामि इति उक्त्वा बन्धुविश्लेषजनितशोकसंविग्नमानसः सशरं चापं विसृज्य रथोपस्थे उपाविशत् ।
commentaries.madhva_sanskrit: ॥ 1.32 ॥ Sri Madhvacharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.11.
commentaries.madhusudana_sanskrit: ॥ 1.32 ॥ ननु माभूददृष्टं प्रयोजनं दृष्टप्रयोजनानि तु विजयो राज्यं सुखानि च निर्विवादानीत्यत आह -- पूर्वत्र सुखं परतः फलाकाङ्क्षा ह्रुपायप्रवृत्तौ कारणम्। अतस्तदाकाङ्क्षाया अभावात्तदुपाये युद्धे भोजनेच्छाविरहिण इव पाकादौ मम प्रवृत्तिरनुपपन्नेत्यर्थः। कुतः पुनरितरपुरुषैरिष्यमाणेषु तेषु तवानिच्छेत्यत आह -- 'किं न इति'। भोगैः सुखैर्जीवितेन जीवितसाधनेन विजयेनेत्यर्थः। विना राज्यं भोगान् कौरवविजयं च वने निवसतामस्माकं तेनैव जगति श्लाघनीयजीवितानां किमेभिराकाङ्क्षितैरिति भावः। गोशब्दवाच्यानीन्द्रियाण्यधिष्ठानतया नित्यं प्राप्तस्त्वमेव ममैहिकफलविरागं जानासीति सूचयन्संबोधयति गोविन्देति। राज्यादीनामाक्षेपे हेतुमाह -- तेन स्वस्य वैराग्येऽपि स्वीयानामर्थे यतनीयमित्यपास्तम्। काकिनो हि राज्याद्यनपेक्षितमेव। येषां तु बन्धूनामर्थे तदपेक्षितं त ते प्राणान्प्राणाशां धनानि धनाशां च त्यक्त्वा युद्धेऽवस्थिता इति न स्वार्थः स्वीयार्थो वायं प्रयत्न इति भावः। भोगशब्दः पूर्वत्र सुखपरतया निर्दिष्टोऽप्यत्र पृथक्सुखग्रहणात्सुखसाधनविषयपरः।
commentaries.sankara_english: 1.32 Sri Sankaracharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.10.
commentaries.ramanuja_english: 1.26 - 1.47 Arjuna said --- Sañjaya said -- Sañjaya continued: The high-minded Arjuna, extremely kind, deeply friendly, and supremely righteous, having brothers like himself, though repeatedly deceived by the treacherous attempts of your people like burning in the lac-house etc., and therefore fit to be killed by him with the help of the Supreme Person, nevertheless said, 'I will not fight.' He felt weak, overcome as he was by his love and extreme compassion for his relatives. He was also filled with fear, not knowing what was righteous and what unrighteous. His mind was tortured by grief, because of the thought of future separation from his relations. So he threw away his bow and arrow and sat on the chariot as if to fast to death.

verses.devanagari:
येषामर्थे काङ्क्षितं नो राज्यं भोगाः सुखानि च।
त इमेऽवस्थिता युद्धे प्राणांस्त्यक्त्वा धनानि च ॥1.33॥
verses.transliteration:
1.33 yeṣām arthe kāṅkṣitaṃ no rājyaṃ bhogāḥ sukhāni ca 
ta ime ‘vasthitā yuddhe prāṇāṃs tyaktvā dhanāni ca

translations.adidevananda: 1.33 Those for whose sake we do desire empire, enjoyment and pleasures, stand here in war renouncing life and wealth ---
translations.gambhirananda: 1.32 -- 1.34 O Govinda! What need do we have of a kingdom, or what (need) of enjoyments and livelihood? Those for whom kingdom, enjoyments and pleasures are desired by us, viz teachers, uncles, fathers-in-law, grandsons, brothers-in-law as also relatives-those very ones stand arrayed for battle risking their lives and wealth.
translations.zaehner: (33) Those for whose sake we covet kingdom, enjoyments, things of pleasure, stand [here arrayed] for battle, surrendering life and wealth—
translations.sivananda: 1.33. Those for whose sake we desire kingdom, enjoyments and pleasures, stand here in battle, having renounced life and wealth.
translations.gemini:
For whose sake a kingdom is desired by us, 
 Enjoyments and pleasures, 
 They are stationed here in battle, 
 Having abandoned their lives and wealth.
translations.stoller_miller:
We sought kingship, delights,
and pleasures for the sake of those
assembled to abandon their lives
and fortunes in battle.

commentaries.ramanuja_sanskrit: ॥ 1.33 ॥ अर्जुन उवाच -- संजय उवाच -- स तु पार्थो महामनाः परमकारुणिको दीर्घबन्धुः परमधार्मिकः सभ्रातृको भवद्भिः अतिघोरैः मारणैः जतुगृहादिभिः असकृद् वञ्चितः अपि परमपुरुषसहायः अपि हनिष्यमाणान् भवदीयान् विलोक्य बन्धुस्नेहेन परमया च कृपया धर्माधर्मभयेन च अतिमात्रस्विन्नसर्वगात्रः सर्वथा अहं न योत्स्यामि इति उक्त्वा बन्धुविश्लेषजनितशोकसंविग्नमानसः सशरं चापं विसृज्य रथोपस्थे उपाविशत् ।
commentaries.madhva_sanskrit: ॥ 1.33 ॥ Sri Madhvacharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.11.
commentaries.madhusudana_sanskrit: ॥ 1.33 ॥ प्राणधनशब्दौ तु तदाशालक्षकौ। स्वप्राणत्यागेऽपि स्वबन्धूनामुपभोगाय धनाशा संभवेदिति तद्वारणाय पृथग्धनग्रहणम्।
commentaries.sankara_english: 1.33 Sri Sankaracharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.10.
commentaries.ramanuja_english: 1.26 - 1.47 Arjuna said --- Sañjaya said -- Sañjaya continued: The high-minded Arjuna, extremely kind, deeply friendly, and supremely righteous, having brothers like himself, though repeatedly deceived by the treacherous attempts of your people like burning in the lac-house etc., and therefore fit to be killed by him with the help of the Supreme Person, nevertheless said, 'I will not fight.' He felt weak, overcome as he was by his love and extreme compassion for his relatives. He was also filled with fear, not knowing what was righteous and what unrighteous. His mind was tortured by grief, because of the thought of future separation from his relations. So he threw away his bow and arrow and sat on the chariot as if to fast to death.

verses.devanagari:
आचार्याः पितरः पुत्रास्तथैव च पितामहाः।
मातुलाः श्चशुराः पौत्राः श्यालाः सम्बन्धिनस्तथा ॥1.34॥
verses.transliteration:
1.34 ācāryāḥ pitaraḥ putrās tathaiva ca pitāmahāḥ 
mātulāḥ śvaśurāḥ pautrāḥ syālāḥ saṃbandhinas tathā

translations.adidevananda: 1.34 Teachers, fathers, sons and also grandfathers, uncles, fathers-in-law and grandsons, brothers-in-law and other kinsmen ---
translations.gambhirananda: 1.32 -- 1.34 O Govinda! What need do we have of a kingdom, or what (need) of enjoyments and livelihood? Those for whom kingdom, enjoyments and pleasures are desired by us, viz teachers, uncles, fathers-in-law, grandsons, brothers-in-law as also relatives-those very ones stand arrayed for battle risking their lives and wealth.
translations.zaehner: (34) teachers, fathers, sons, and grandsires too; uncles, fathers-in-law, grandsons, brothers-in-law—kinsmen all.
translations.sivananda: 1.34. Teachers, fathers, sons and also grandfathers, maternal uncles, fathers-in-law, grandsons, brothers-in-law and other relatives,-
translations.gemini:
Teachers (ācāryāḥ), fathers, sons, 
 And similarly grandfathers, 
 Maternal uncles, fathers-in-law, grandsons, 
 Brothers-in-law, and relatives as well.
translations.stoller_miller:
They are teachers, fathers, sons,
and grandfathers, uncles, grandsons,
fathers and brothers of wives,
and other men of our family.

commentaries.ramanuja_sanskrit: ॥ 1.34 ॥ अर्जुन उवाच -- संजय उवाच -- स तु पार्थो महामनाः परमकारुणिको दीर्घबन्धुः परमधार्मिकः सभ्रातृको भवद्भिः अतिघोरैः मारणैः जतुगृहादिभिः असकृद् वञ्चितः अपि परमपुरुषसहायः अपि हनिष्यमाणान् भवदीयान् विलोक्य बन्धुस्नेहेन परमया च कृपया धर्माधर्मभयेन च अतिमात्रस्विन्नसर्वगात्रः सर्वथा अहं न योत्स्यामि इति उक्त्वा बन्धुविश्लेषजनितशोकसंविग्नमानसः सशरं चापं विसृज्य रथोपस्थे उपाविशत् ।
commentaries.madhva_sanskrit: ॥ 1.34 ॥ Sri Madhvacharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.11.
commentaries.madhusudana_sanskrit: ॥ 1.34 ॥ येषामर्थे राज्याद्यपेक्षितं तेऽत्र नागता इत्याशङ्क्य तान्विशिनष्टि स्पष्टम्। ननु यदि कृपया त्वमेतान्न हंसि तर्हि त्वामेते राज्यलोभेन हनिष्यन्त्येव अतस्त्वमेवैतान्हत्वा राज्यं भुङ्क्ष्वेत्यत आह -- त्रैलोक्यराज्यस्यापि हेतोः तत्प्राप्त्यर्थमपि, अस्मान्घ्नतोऽप्येतान्न हन्तुमिच्छामि इच्छामपि न कुर्यामहं किं पुनर्हन्याम्, महीमात्रप्राप्तये तु न हन्यामिति किमु वक्तव्यमित्यर्थः। मधुसूदनेति संबोधयन्वैदिकमार्गप्रवर्तकत्वं भगवतः सूचयति।
commentaries.sankara_english: 1.34 Sri Sankaracharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.10.
commentaries.ramanuja_english: 1.26 - 1.47 Arjuna said --- Sañjaya said -- Sañjaya continued: The high-minded Arjuna, extremely kind, deeply friendly, and supremely righteous, having brothers like himself, though repeatedly deceived by the treacherous attempts of your people like burning in the lac-house etc., and therefore fit to be killed by him with the help of the Supreme Person, nevertheless said, 'I will not fight.' He felt weak, overcome as he was by his love and extreme compassion for his relatives. He was also filled with fear, not knowing what was righteous and what unrighteous. His mind was tortured by grief, because of the thought of future separation from his relations. So he threw away his bow and arrow and sat on the chariot as if to fast to death.

verses.devanagari:
एतान्न हन्तुमिच्छामि घ्नतोऽपि मधुसूदन।
अपि त्रैलोक्यराज्यस्य हेतोः किं नु महीकृते ॥1.35॥
verses.transliteration:
1.35 etān na hantum icchāmi ghnato ‘pi madhusūdana 
api trailokyarājyasya hetoḥ kiṃ nu mahīkṛte

translations.adidevananda: 1.35 These I would not slay, though they might slay me, even for the sovereignty of the three worlds --- how much less for this earth O Kṛṣṇa?
translations.gambhirananda: 1.35 O Madhusūdana, even if I am killed, I do not want to kill these even for the sake of a kingdom extending over the three worlds; what to speak of doing so for the earth!
translations.zaehner: (35) Kṛṣṇa, though they should slay [me], yet would not I slay them, not for the dominion over the three worlds, how much less for the earth [alone]!
translations.sivananda: 1.35. These I do not wish to kill, though they kill me, O Kṛṣṇa, even for the sake of dominion over the three worlds; leave alone killing them for the sake of the earth.
translations.gemini:
These I do not wish to kill, 
 Even though they kill me, O Slayer of Madhu (Madhusūdana). 
 Not even for the sake of the kingdom of the three worlds, 
 How much less for the earth!
translations.stoller_miller:
I do not want to kill them
even if I am killed, Krishna;
not for kingship of all three worlds,
much less for the earth!

commentaries.ramanuja_sanskrit: ॥ 1.35 ॥ अर्जुन उवाच -- संजय उवाच -- स तु पार्थो महामनाः परमकारुणिको दीर्घबन्धुः परमधार्मिकः सभ्रातृको भवद्भिः अतिघोरैः मारणैः जतुगृहादिभिः असकृद् वञ्चितः अपि परमपुरुषसहायः अपि हनिष्यमाणान् भवदीयान् विलोक्य बन्धुस्नेहेन परमया च कृपया धर्माधर्मभयेन च अतिमात्रस्विन्नसर्वगात्रः सर्वथा अहं न योत्स्यामि इति उक्त्वा बन्धुविश्लेषजनितशोकसंविग्नमानसः सशरं चापं विसृज्य रथोपस्थे उपाविशत् ।
commentaries.madhva_sanskrit: ॥ 1.35 ॥ Sri Madhvacharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.11.
commentaries.madhusudana_sanskrit: ॥ 1.35 ॥ नन्वंन्यान्विहाय धार्तराष्ट्रा व हन्तव्यास्तेषामत्यन्तक्रूरतरतत्तद्दुःखदातृणां वधे प्रीतिसंभवादित्यत आह -- धार्तराष्ट्रान्दुर्योधनादीन्भ्रातॄन्निहत्य स्थितनामस्माकं का प्रीतिः स्यात्। न कापीत्यर्थः। नहि मूढजनोचितक्षणमात्रवर्तिसुखाभासलोभेन चिरतरनरकयातनाहेतुर्बन्धुवधोऽस्माकं युक्त इति भावः। जनार्दनेति संबोधनेन यदि वध्या ते तर्हि त्वमेवैताञ्जहि, प्रलये सर्वजनहिंसकत्वेऽपि सर्वपापासंसर्गित्वादिति सूचयति। ननु'अग्निदो गरदश्चैव शस्त्रपाणिर्धनापहः। क्षेत्रदारहरश्चैव षडेते ह्राततायिनः ॥ ' इति स्मृतेरेतेषां च सर्वप्रकारैराततायित्वात्'आतताययिनमायान्तं हन्यादेवाविचारयन्। नाततायिवधे दोषो हन्तुर्भवति कश्चन ॥ ' इति वचनेन दोषाभावप्रतीतेर्हन्तव्या व दुर्योधनादय आततायिन इत्याशङ्क्याह -- 'पापमेवेति'। तानाततायिनोऽपि हत्वा स्थितानस्मान्पापमाश्रयेदेवेति संबन्धः। अथवा पापमेवाश्रयेन्न किंचिदन्यद्दृष्टदृष्टं वा प्रयोजनमित्यर्थः।'न हिंस्यात्' इति धर्मशास्त्रात्'आततायिनं हन्यात्' इत्यर्थशास्त्रस्य दुर्बलत्वात्। तदुक्तं याज्ञवल्क्येन -- 'स्मृत्योर्विरोधे न्यायस्तु बलवान्व्यवहारतः। अर्थशास्त्रात्तु बलवद्धर्मशास्त्रमिति स्थितिः ॥ ' इति।'अपरा व्याख्या'। ननु धार्ताराष्ट्रान्घ्नतां भवतां प्रीत्यभावेऽपि युष्मान्घ्नतां धार्तराष्ट्राणां प्रीतिरस्त्येवातस्ते युष्मान्हन्युरित्यत आह -- 'पापमेवेति'। अस्मान्हत्वा स्थितानेतानाततायिनो धार्ताराष्ट्रान्पूर्वमपि पापिनः सांप्रतमपि पापमेवाश्रयेन्नान्यÏत्कचित्सुखमित्यर्थः। तथा चायुध्यतोऽस्मान्हत्वैत व पापिनो भविष्यन्ति नास्माकं कापि क्षतिः पापासंबन्धादित्यभिप्रायः।
commentaries.sankara_english: 1.35 Sri Sankaracharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.10.
commentaries.ramanuja_english: 1.26 - 1.47 Arjuna said --- Sañjaya said -- Sañjaya continued: The high-minded Arjuna, extremely kind, deeply friendly, and supremely righteous, having brothers like himself, though repeatedly deceived by the treacherous attempts of your people like burning in the lac-house etc., and therefore fit to be killed by him with the help of the Supreme Person, nevertheless said, 'I will not fight.' He felt weak, overcome as he was by his love and extreme compassion for his relatives. He was also filled with fear, not knowing what was righteous and what unrighteous. His mind was tortured by grief, because of the thought of future separation from his relations. So he threw away his bow and arrow and sat on the chariot as if to fast to death.

verses.devanagari:
निहत्य धार्तराष्ट्रान्नः का प्रीतिः स्याज्जनार्दन।
पापमेवाश्रयेदस्मान्हत्वैतानाततायिनः ॥1.36॥
verses.transliteration:
1.36 nihatya dhārtarāṣṭrān naḥ kā prītiḥ syāj janārdana 
pāpam evāśrayed asmān hatvaitān ātatāyinaḥ

translations.adidevananda: 1.36 If we kill the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, what joy will be ours, O Kṛṣṇa? Sin alone will accrue to us if we kill these murderous felons.
translations.gambhirananda: 1.36 O Janārdana, what happiness shall we derive by killing the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra? Sin alone will accrue to us by killing these felons.
translations.zaehner: (36) Should we slaughter Dhṛtarāṣṭra’s sons, Kṛṣṇa, what sweetness then is ours? Evil, and only evil, would come to dwell with us, should we slay them, hate us as they may.
translations.sivananda: 1.36. By killing these sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, what pleasure can be ours, O Janārdana? Only sin will accrue to us from killing these felons.
translations.gemini:
Having killed the sons of Dhritarashtra, 
 What pleasure would there be for us, O Agitator of men (Janārdana)? 
 Sin (pāpa) alone would take hold of us, 
 Having killed these aggressors.
translations.stoller_miller:
What joy is there for us, Krishna,
in killing Dhritarashtra's sons?
Evil will haunt us if we kill them,
though their bows are drawn to kill.

commentaries.ramanuja_sanskrit: ॥ 1.36 ॥ अर्जुन उवाच -- संजय उवाच -- स तु पार्थो महामनाः परमकारुणिको दीर्घबन्धुः परमधार्मिकः सभ्रातृको भवद्भिः अतिघोरैः मारणैः जतुगृहादिभिः असकृद् वञ्चितः अपि परमपुरुषसहायः अपि हनिष्यमाणान् भवदीयान् विलोक्य बन्धुस्नेहेन परमया च कृपया धर्माधर्मभयेन च अतिमात्रस्विन्नसर्वगात्रः सर्वथा अहं न योत्स्यामि इति उक्त्वा बन्धुविश्लेषजनितशोकसंविग्नमानसः सशरं चापं विसृज्य रथोपस्थे उपाविशत् ।
commentaries.madhva_sanskrit: ॥ 1.36 ॥ Sri Madhvacharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.11.
commentaries.madhusudana_sanskrit: ॥ 1.36 ॥ फलाभावदनर्थसंभवाच्च परहिंसा न कर्तव्येति'नच श्रेयोऽनुपश्यामि' इत्यारभ्योक्तं तदुपसंहरति। अदृष्टफलाभावोऽनर्थसंभवश्च तच्छब्देन परामृश्यते। दृष्टसुखाभावमाह -- 'स्वजनं हीति'। माधवेति लक्ष्मीपतित्वान्नालक्ष्मीके कर्मणि प्रवर्तयितुमर्हसीति भावः।
commentaries.sankara_english: 1.36 Sri Sankaracharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.10.
commentaries.ramanuja_english: 1.26 - 1.47 Arjuna said --- Sañjaya said -- Sañjaya continued: The high-minded Arjuna, extremely kind, deeply friendly, and supremely righteous, having brothers like himself, though repeatedly deceived by the treacherous attempts of your people like burning in the lac-house etc., and therefore fit to be killed by him with the help of the Supreme Person, nevertheless said, 'I will not fight.' He felt weak, overcome as he was by his love and extreme compassion for his relatives. He was also filled with fear, not knowing what was righteous and what unrighteous. His mind was tortured by grief, because of the thought of future separation from his relations. So he threw away his bow and arrow and sat on the chariot as if to fast to death.

verses.devanagari:
तस्मान्नार्हा वयं हन्तुं धार्तराष्ट्रान्स्वबान्धवान्।
स्वजनं हि कथं हत्वा सुखिनः स्याम माधव ॥1.37॥
verses.transliteration:
1.37 tasmān nārhā vayaṃ hantuṃ dhārtarāṣṭrān sabāndhavān 
svajanaṃ hi kathaṃ hatvā sukhinaḥ syāma mādhava

translations.adidevananda: 1.37 Therefore, it is not befitting that we slay our kin, the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra. For if we kill our kinsmen, O Kṛṣṇa, how indeed can we be happy?
translations.gambhirananda: 1.37 Therefore, it is not proper for us to kill the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra who are our own relatives. For, O Mādhava, how can we be happy by killing our kinsmen?
translations.zaehner: (37) Therefore we have no right to kill the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra and their kin. For, Kṛṣṇa, were we to lay low our own folk, how could we be happy?
translations.sivananda: 1.37. Therefore, we should not kill the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, our relatives; for how can we be happy by killing our own people, O Mādhava (Kṛṣṇa)?
translations.gemini:
Therefore, it is not fitting that we kill 
 The sons of Dhritarashtra, our own kinsmen. 
 For having killed our own people, how, 
 Could we be happy, O Lord of fortune (Mādhava)?
translations.stoller_miller:
Honor forbids us to kill
our cousins, Dhritarashtra's sons;
how can we know happiness
if we kill our own kinsmen?

commentaries.ramanuja_sanskrit: ॥ 1.37 ॥ अर्जुन उवाच -- संजय उवाच -- स तु पार्थो महामनाः परमकारुणिको दीर्घबन्धुः परमधार्मिकः सभ्रातृको भवद्भिः अतिघोरैः मारणैः जतुगृहादिभिः असकृद् वञ्चितः अपि परमपुरुषसहायः अपि हनिष्यमाणान् भवदीयान् विलोक्य बन्धुस्नेहेन परमया च कृपया धर्माधर्मभयेन च अतिमात्रस्विन्नसर्वगात्रः सर्वथा अहं न योत्स्यामि इति उक्त्वा बन्धुविश्लेषजनितशोकसंविग्नमानसः सशरं चापं विसृज्य रथोपस्थे उपाविशत् ।
commentaries.madhva_sanskrit: ॥ 1.37 ॥ Sri Madhvacharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.11.
commentaries.madhusudana_sanskrit: ॥ 1.37 ॥ कथं तर्हि परेषां कुलक्षये स्वजनहिंसायां च प्रवृत्तिस्तत्राह -- लोभोपहतबुद्धित्वात्तेषां कुलक्षयादिनिमित्तदोषप्रतिसंधानाभावात्प्रवृत्तिः संभवतीत्यर्थः। अतव भीष्मादीनां शिष्टानां बन्धुवधे प्रवृत्तत्वाच्छिष्टाचारत्वेन वेदमूलत्वादितरेषामपि तत्प्रवृत्तिरुचितेत्यपास्तम।'हेतुदर्शनाच्च' इति न्यायात्। तत्रहि लोभादिहेतुदर्शने वेदमूलत्वं न कल्प्यत इति स्थापितं यद्यप्येते न पश्यन्ति तथापि कथमस्माभिर्न ज्ञेयमित्युत्तरश्लोकेन संबन्धः।
commentaries.sankara_english: 1.37 Sri Sankaracharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.10.
commentaries.ramanuja_english: 1.26 - 1.47 Arjuna said --- Sañjaya said -- Sañjaya continued: The high-minded Arjuna, extremely kind, deeply friendly, and supremely righteous, having brothers like himself, though repeatedly deceived by the treacherous attempts of your people like burning in the lac-house etc., and therefore fit to be killed by him with the help of the Supreme Person, nevertheless said, 'I will not fight.' He felt weak, overcome as he was by his love and extreme compassion for his relatives. He was also filled with fear, not knowing what was righteous and what unrighteous. His mind was tortured by grief, because of the thought of future separation from his relations. So he threw away his bow and arrow and sat on the chariot as if to fast to death.

verses.devanagari:
यद्यप्येते न पश्यन्ति लोभोपहतचेतसः।
कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं मित्रद्रोहे च पातकम् ॥1.38॥
verses.transliteration:
1.38 yady apy ete na paśyanti lobhopahatacetasaḥ 
kulakṣayakṛtaṃ doṣaṃ mitradrohe ca pātakam

translations.adidevananda: 1.38 Though these people, whose minds are overpowered by greed, see no evil in the destruction of a clan and no sin in treachery to friends,
translations.gambhirananda: 1.38 -- 1.39 O Janārdana, although these people, whose hearts have become perverted by greed, do not see the evil arising from destroying the family and sin in hostility towards, friends, yet how can we who clearly see the evil arising from destroying the family remain unaware of (the need of) abstaining from this sin?
translations.zaehner: (38) And even if, bereft of sense by grief, they cannot see that to ruin a family is wickedness and to break one's word a crime,
translations.sivananda: 1.38. Though they, with intelligence overpowered by greed, see no evil in the destruction of families, and no sin in hostility to friends,
translations.gemini:
Even if these do not see, 
 Their minds overcome by greed, 
 The fault (doṣa) caused by the destruction of the family (kula), 
 And the crime (pātaka) in treachery to friends,
translations.stoller_miller:
The greed that distorts their reason
blinds them to the sin they commit
in ruining the family, blinds them
to the crime of betraying friends.

commentaries.ramanuja_sanskrit: ॥ 1.38 ॥ अर्जुन उवाच -- संजय उवाच -- स तु पार्थो महामनाः परमकारुणिको दीर्घबन्धुः परमधार्मिकः सभ्रातृको भवद्भिः अतिघोरैः मारणैः जतुगृहादिभिः असकृद् वञ्चितः अपि परमपुरुषसहायः अपि हनिष्यमाणान् भवदीयान् विलोक्य बन्धुस्नेहेन परमया च कृपया धर्माधर्मभयेन च अतिमात्रस्विन्नसर्वगात्रः सर्वथा अहं न योत्स्यामि इति उक्त्वा बन्धुविश्लेषजनितशोकसंविग्नमानसः सशरं चापं विसृज्य रथोपस्थे उपाविशत् ।
commentaries.madhva_sanskrit: ॥ 1.38 ॥ Sri Madhvacharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.11.
commentaries.madhusudana_sanskrit: ॥ 1.38 ॥ ननु यद्यप्येते लोभात्प्रवृत्तास्तथापि'आहूतो न निवर्तेत द्यूतादपि रणदपि' इति'विजितं क्षत्रियस्य' इत्यादिभिः क्षत्रियस्य युद्धं धर्मो युद्धार्जितं च धम्र्यं धनमिति शास्त्रे निश्चयाद्भवतां च तैराहूतत्वाद्युद्धे प्रवृत्तिरूचितैवेत्याशङ्क्याह -- अस्मात्पापाद्वन्धुवधफलकयुद्धरूपात्। अयमर्थः -- श्रेयःसाधनताज्ञानं हि प्रवर्तकं श्रेयश्च तद्यदश्रेयोऽननुबन्धि। अन्यथा श्येनादीनामपि धर्मत्वापत्तेः। तथाचोक्तम्'फलतोऽपि च यत्कर्म नानर्थेनानुबध्यते। केवलप्रीतिहेतुत्वात्तद्धर्म इति कथ्यते ॥ ' इति, ततश्चाश्रेयोनुबन्धितया शास्त्रप्रतिपादितेऽपि श्येनादाविवास्मिन्युद्धेऽपि नास्माकं प्रवृत्तिरुचितेति।
commentaries.sankara_english: 1.38 Sri Sankaracharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.10.
commentaries.ramanuja_english: 1.26 - 1.47 Arjuna said --- Sañjaya said -- Sañjaya continued: The high-minded Arjuna, extremely kind, deeply friendly, and supremely righteous, having brothers like himself, though repeatedly deceived by the treacherous attempts of your people like burning in the lac-house etc., and therefore fit to be killed by him with the help of the Supreme Person, nevertheless said, 'I will not fight.' He felt weak, overcome as he was by his love and extreme compassion for his relatives. He was also filled with fear, not knowing what was righteous and what unrighteous. His mind was tortured by grief, because of the thought of future separation from his relations. So he threw away his bow and arrow and sat on the chariot as if to fast to death.

verses.devanagari:
कथं न ज्ञेयमस्माभिः पापादस्मान्निवर्तितुम्।
कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं प्रपश्यद्भिर्जनार्दन ॥1.39॥
verses.transliteration:
1.39 kathaṃ na jñeyam asmābhiḥ pāpād asmān nivartitum 
kulakṣayakṛtaṃ doṣaṃ prapaśyadbhir janārdana

translations.adidevananda: 1.39 Why should we not learn to shun this crime --- we who see the evil of ruining a clan, O Kṛṣṇa?
translations.gambhirananda: 1.38 -- 1.39 O Janārdana, although these people, whose hearts have become perverted by greed, do not see the evil arising from destroying the family and sin in hostility towards, friends, yet how can we who clearly see the evil arising from destroying the family remain unaware of (the need of) abstaining from this sin?
translations.zaehner: (39) how should we not be wise enough to shun this evil thing, for we clearly see that to ruin a family is wickedness.
translations.sivananda: 1.39. Why should not we who clearly see evil in the destruction of families, learn to turn away from this sin, O Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa)?
translations.gemini:
How should it not be known by us, 
 To turn away from this sin (pāpa), 
 The fault (doṣa) caused by the destruction of the family (kula), 
 Clearly seeing it, O Agitator of men (Janārdana)?
translations.stoller_miller:
How can we ignore the wisdom
of turning from this evil
when we see the sin
of family destruction, Krishna?

commentaries.ramanuja_sanskrit: ॥ 1.39 ॥ अर्जुन उवाच -- संजय उवाच -- स तु पार्थो महामनाः परमकारुणिको दीर्घबन्धुः परमधार्मिकः सभ्रातृको भवद्भिः अतिघोरैः मारणैः जतुगृहादिभिः असकृद् वञ्चितः अपि परमपुरुषसहायः अपि हनिष्यमाणान् भवदीयान् विलोक्य बन्धुस्नेहेन परमया च कृपया धर्माधर्मभयेन च अतिमात्रस्विन्नसर्वगात्रः सर्वथा अहं न योत्स्यामि इति उक्त्वा बन्धुविश्लेषजनितशोकसंविग्नमानसः सशरं चापं विसृज्य रथोपस्थे उपाविशत् ।
commentaries.madhva_sanskrit: ॥ 1.39 ॥ Sri Madhvacharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.11.
commentaries.madhusudana_sanskrit: ॥ 1.39 ॥ वंच विजयादीनामश्रेयस्त्वेनानाकाङ्क्षितत्वान्न तदर्थं प्रवृर्तितव्यमिति द्रढयितुमनर्थानुबन्धित्वेनाश्रेयस्त्वमेव प्रपञ्चयन्नाह -- सनातनाः परम्पराप्राप्ताः कुलधर्माः कुलोचिता धर्माः कुलक्षये प्रणश्यन्ति कर्तुरभावात्। उत अपि अग्निहोत्राद्यनुष्ठातृपुरुषनाशेन धर्मे नष्टे। जात्यभिप्रायमेकवचनम्। अवशिष्टं बालादिरूपं कृत्स्नमपि कुलधर्मोऽभिभवति स्वाधीनतया व्याप्नोति। उतशब्दः कृत्स्नपदेन संबध्यते।
commentaries.sankara_english: 1.39 Sri Sankaracharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.10.
commentaries.ramanuja_english: 1.26 - 1.47 Arjuna said --- Sañjaya said -- Sañjaya continued: The high-minded Arjuna, extremely kind, deeply friendly, and supremely righteous, having brothers like himself, though repeatedly deceived by the treacherous attempts of your people like burning in the lac-house etc., and therefore fit to be killed by him with the help of the Supreme Person, nevertheless said, 'I will not fight.' He felt weak, overcome as he was by his love and extreme compassion for his relatives. He was also filled with fear, not knowing what was righteous and what unrighteous. His mind was tortured by grief, because of the thought of future separation from his relations. So he threw away his bow and arrow and sat on the chariot as if to fast to death.

verses.devanagari:
कुलक्षये प्रणश्यन्ति कुलधर्माः सनातनाः।
धर्मे नष्टे कुलं कृत्स्नमधर्मोऽभिभवत्युत ॥1.40॥
verses.transliteration:
1.40 kulakṣaye praṇaśyanti kuladharmāḥ sanātanāḥ 
dharme naṣṭe kulaṃ kṛtsnam adharmo ‘bhibhavaty uta

translations.adidevananda: 1.40 With the ruin of a clan, perish its ancient traditions, and when traditions perish, lawlessness overtakes the whole clan.
translations.gambhirananda: 1.40 From the ruin of the family are totally destroyed the traditional rites and duties of the family. When rites and duties are destroyed, vice overpowers the entire family also.
translations.zaehner: (40) Once the family is ruined, the primeval family laws collapse. Once law is destroyed, then lawlessness overwhelms all [that is known as] family.
translations.sivananda: 1.40. In the destruction of a family, the immemorial religious rites of that family perish; on the destruction of spirituality, impiety, indeed, overcomes the whole family.
translations.gemini:
In the destruction of the family (kula), perish 
 The eternal laws of the family (kuladharmāḥ); 
 When law (dharma) is destroyed, the entire family, 
 Is also overcome by lawlessness (adharma).
translations.stoller_miller:
When the family is ruined,
the timeless laws of family duty
perish; and when duty is lost,
chaos overwhelms the family.

commentaries.ramanuja_sanskrit: ॥ 1.40 ॥ अर्जुन उवाच -- संजय उवाच -- स तु पार्थो महामनाः परमकारुणिको दीर्घबन्धुः परमधार्मिकः सभ्रातृको भवद्भिः अतिघोरैः मारणैः जतुगृहादिभिः असकृद् वञ्चितः अपि परमपुरुषसहायः अपि हनिष्यमाणान् भवदीयान् विलोक्य बन्धुस्नेहेन परमया च कृपया धर्माधर्मभयेन च अतिमात्रस्विन्नसर्वगात्रः सर्वथा अहं न योत्स्यामि इति उक्त्वा बन्धुविश्लेषजनितशोकसंविग्नमानसः सशरं चापं विसृज्य रथोपस्थे उपाविशत् ।
commentaries.madhva_sanskrit: ॥ 1.40 ॥ Sri Madhvacharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.11.
commentaries.madhusudana_sanskrit: ॥ 1.40 ॥ अस्मदीयैः पतिभिर्धर्मतिक्रम्य कुलक्षयः कृतश्चेदस्माभिरपि व्यभिचारे कृते को दोषः स्यादित्येवं कुतर्कहताः कुलस्त्रियः प्रदुष्येयुरित्यर्थः। अथवा कुलक्षयकारिपतितपतिसंबन्धादेव स्त्रीणां दुष्टत्वम्'आशुद्धेः संप्रतीक्ष्यो हि महापातकदूषितः' इत्यादिस्मृतेः।
commentaries.sankara_english: 1.40 Sri Sankaracharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.10.
commentaries.ramanuja_english: 1.26 - 1.47 Arjuna said --- Sañjaya said -- Sañjaya continued: The high-minded Arjuna, extremely kind, deeply friendly, and supremely righteous, having brothers like himself, though repeatedly deceived by the treacherous attempts of your people like burning in the lac-house etc., and therefore fit to be killed by him with the help of the Supreme Person, nevertheless said, 'I will not fight.' He felt weak, overcome as he was by his love and extreme compassion for his relatives. He was also filled with fear, not knowing what was righteous and what unrighteous. His mind was tortured by grief, because of the thought of future separation from his relations. So he threw away his bow and arrow and sat on the chariot as if to fast to death.

verses.devanagari:
अधर्माभिभवात्कृष्ण प्रदुष्यन्ति कुलस्त्रियः।
स्त्रीषु दुष्टासु वाष्र्णेय जायते वर्णसङ्करः ॥1.41॥
verses.transliteration:
1.41 adharmābhibhavāt kṛṣṇa praduṣyanti kulastriyaḥ 
strīṣu duṣṭāsu vārṣṇeya jāyate varṇasaṃkaraḥ

translations.adidevananda: 1.41 When lawlessness prevails, O Kṛṣṇa, the women of the clan become corrupt; when women become corrupt, there arises intermixture of classes.
translations.gambhirananda: 1.41 O Kṛṣṇa, when vice predominates, the women of the family become corrupt. O descendent of the Vṛṣṇis, when women become corrupted, it results in the intermingling of castes.
translations.zaehner: (41) With lawlessness triumphant, Kṛṣṇa, the family's women are debauched; once the women are debauched, there will be a mixing of caste.
translations.sivananda: 1.41. By the prevalence of impiety, O Kṛṣṇa, the women of the family become corrupt; and , women being corrupted, O Vārṣṇeya (descendant of Vṛṣṇī), there arises intermingling of castes.
translations.gemini:
From the overcoming by lawlessness (adharma), O Dark one (Kṛṣṇa), 
 The women of the family become corrupted; 
 When the women are corrupted, O Descendant of Vrishni (Vārṣṇeya), 
 The mixing of castes (varṇasaṅkara) arises.
translations.stoller_miller:
In overwhelming chaos, Krishna,
women of the family are corrupted;
and when women are corrupted,
disorder is born in society.

commentaries.ramanuja_sanskrit: ॥ 1.41 ॥ अर्जुन उवाच -- संजय उवाच -- स तु पार्थो महामनाः परमकारुणिको दीर्घबन्धुः परमधार्मिकः सभ्रातृको भवद्भिः अतिघोरैः मारणैः जतुगृहादिभिः असकृद् वञ्चितः अपि परमपुरुषसहायः अपि हनिष्यमाणान् भवदीयान् विलोक्य बन्धुस्नेहेन परमया च कृपया धर्माधर्मभयेन च अतिमात्रस्विन्नसर्वगात्रः सर्वथा अहं न योत्स्यामि इति उक्त्वा बन्धुविश्लेषजनितशोकसंविग्नमानसः सशरं चापं विसृज्य रथोपस्थे उपाविशत् ।
commentaries.madhva_sanskrit: ॥ 1.41 ॥ Sri Madhvacharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.11.
commentaries.madhusudana_sanskrit: ॥ 1.41 ॥ कुलस्य संकरश्च कुलघ्नानां नरकायैव भवतीत्यन्वयः। न केवलं कुलघ्नानामेव नरकपातः किंतु तत्पितॄणामपीत्याह -- 'पतन्तीति'। हिशब्दोऽप्यर्थे हेतौ वा। पुत्रादीनां कतर्ॄणामभावाल्लुप्ता पिण्डस्योदकस्य च क्रिया येषां ते तथा कुलघ्नानां पितरः पतन्ति नरकायैवेत्यनुषङ्गः।
commentaries.sankara_english: 1.41 Sri Sankaracharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.10.
commentaries.ramanuja_english: 1.26 - 1.47 Arjuna said --- Sañjaya said -- Sañjaya continued: The high-minded Arjuna, extremely kind, deeply friendly, and supremely righteous, having brothers like himself, though repeatedly deceived by the treacherous attempts of your people like burning in the lac-house etc., and therefore fit to be killed by him with the help of the Supreme Person, nevertheless said, 'I will not fight.' He felt weak, overcome as he was by his love and extreme compassion for his relatives. He was also filled with fear, not knowing what was righteous and what unrighteous. His mind was tortured by grief, because of the thought of future separation from his relations. So he threw away his bow and arrow and sat on the chariot as if to fast to death.

verses.devanagari:
सङ्करो नरकायैव कुलघ्नानां कुलस्य च।
पतन्ति पितरो ह्रेषां लुप्तपिण्डोदकक्रियाः ॥1.42॥
verses.transliteration:
1.42 saṃkaro narakāyaiva kulaghnānāṃ kulasya ca 
patanti pitaro hy eṣāṃ luptapiṇḍodakakriyāḥ

translations.adidevananda: 1.42 This mixing of classes leads to hell the clan itself and its destroyers; for the spirits of their ancestors fall degraded, deprived of the ritual offerings of food and water.
translations.gambhirananda: 1.42 And the intermingling in the family leads the ruiners of the family verily into hell. The forefathers of these fall down (into hell) because of being deprived of the offerings of rice-balls and water.
translations.zaehner: (42) The mixing of caste leads to hell—[the hell prepared] for those who wreck the family and for the family [so wrecked]. So too their ancestors fall down [to hell], cheated of their offerings of food and drink.
translations.sivananda: 1.42. Confusion of castes leads to hell the slayers of the family, for their forefathers fall, deprived of the offerings of rice-ball and water (libations).
translations.gemini:
The mixing (saṅkara) leads to hell indeed, 
 For the destroyers of the family and for the family; 
 For their ancestors (pitaraḥ) fall, 
 Deprived of the offerings of rice-balls and water.
translations.stoller_miller:
This discord drags the violators
and the family itself to hell;
for ancestors fall when rites
of offering rice and water lapse.

commentaries.ramanuja_sanskrit: ॥ 1.42 ॥ अर्जुन उवाच -- संजय उवाच -- स तु पार्थो महामनाः परमकारुणिको दीर्घबन्धुः परमधार्मिकः सभ्रातृको भवद्भिः अतिघोरैः मारणैः जतुगृहादिभिः असकृद् वञ्चितः अपि परमपुरुषसहायः अपि हनिष्यमाणान् भवदीयान् विलोक्य बन्धुस्नेहेन परमया च कृपया धर्माधर्मभयेन च अतिमात्रस्विन्नसर्वगात्रः सर्वथा अहं न योत्स्यामि इति उक्त्वा बन्धुविश्लेषजनितशोकसंविग्नमानसः सशरं चापं विसृज्य रथोपस्थे उपाविशत् ।
commentaries.madhva_sanskrit: ॥ 1.42 ॥ Sri Madhvacharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.11.
commentaries.madhusudana_sanskrit: ॥ 1.42 ॥ जातिधर्माः क्षत्रियत्वादिनिबन्धनाः, कुलधर्मा असाधारणाश्च तैर्दोषैरुत्साद्यन्ते उत्सन्नाः क्रियन्ते। विनाश्यन्त इत्यर्थः।
commentaries.sankara_english: 1.42 Sri Sankaracharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.10.
commentaries.ramanuja_english: 1.26 - 1.47 Arjuna said --- Sañjaya said -- Sañjaya continued: The high-minded Arjuna, extremely kind, deeply friendly, and supremely righteous, having brothers like himself, though repeatedly deceived by the treacherous attempts of your people like burning in the lac-house etc., and therefore fit to be killed by him with the help of the Supreme Person, nevertheless said, 'I will not fight.' He felt weak, overcome as he was by his love and extreme compassion for his relatives. He was also filled with fear, not knowing what was righteous and what unrighteous. His mind was tortured by grief, because of the thought of future separation from his relations. So he threw away his bow and arrow and sat on the chariot as if to fast to death.

verses.devanagari:
दोषैरेतैः कुलघ्नानां वर्णसङ्करकारकैः।
उत्साद्यन्ते जातिधर्माः कुलधर्माश्च शाश्वताः ॥1.43॥
verses.transliteration:
1.43 doṣair etaiḥ kulaghnānāṃ varṇasaṃkarakārakaiḥ 
utsādyante jātidharmāḥ kuladharmāś ca śāśvatāḥ

translations.adidevananda: 1.43 By the sins of the clan-destroyers who bring about inter-mixture of classes, the ancient traditions of the clan and class are destroyed.
translations.gambhirananda: 1.43 Due to these misdeeds of the ruiners of the family, which cause intermingling of castes, the traditional rites and duties of the castes and families become destroyed.
translations.zaehner: (43) These evil ways of men who wreck the family, [these evil ways] that cause the mixing of caste, [these evil ways] bring caste-law to naught and the eternal family laws.
translations.sivananda: 1.43. By these evil deeds of the destroyers of the family, which cause confusion of castes, the eternal religious rites of the caste and the family are destroyed.
translations.gemini:
By these faults (doṣa) of the destroyers of the family, 
 Which cause the mixing of castes (varṇasaṅkara), 
 Are destroyed the eternal laws of the caste (jātidharmāḥ), 
 And the laws of the family (kuladharmāḥ).
translations.stoller_miller:
The sins of men who violate
the family create disorder in society
that undermines the constant laws
of caste and family duty.

commentaries.ramanuja_sanskrit: ॥ 1.43 ॥ अर्जुन उवाच -- संजय उवाच -- स तु पार्थो महामनाः परमकारुणिको दीर्घबन्धुः परमधार्मिकः सभ्रातृको भवद्भिः अतिघोरैः मारणैः जतुगृहादिभिः असकृद् वञ्चितः अपि परमपुरुषसहायः अपि हनिष्यमाणान् भवदीयान् विलोक्य बन्धुस्नेहेन परमया च कृपया धर्माधर्मभयेन च अतिमात्रस्विन्नसर्वगात्रः सर्वथा अहं न योत्स्यामि इति उक्त्वा बन्धुविश्लेषजनितशोकसंविग्नमानसः सशरं चापं विसृज्य रथोपस्थे उपाविशत् ।
commentaries.madhva_sanskrit: ॥ 1.43 ॥ Sri Madhvacharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.11.
commentaries.madhusudana_sanskrit: ॥ 1.43 ॥ ततश्च प्रेतत्वपरावृत्तिकारणाभावान्नरके व केवलं निरन्तरं वासो भवति ध्रुवमित्यनुशुश्रुमेत्याचार्याणां मुखाद्वयं श्रुतवन्तो न स्वाभ्यूहेन कल्पयाम इति पूर्वोक्तस्यैव दृढीकरणम्।
commentaries.sankara_english: 1.43 Sri Sankaracharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.10.
commentaries.ramanuja_english: 1.26 - 1.47 Arjuna said --- Sañjaya said -- Sañjaya continued: The high-minded Arjuna, extremely kind, deeply friendly, and supremely righteous, having brothers like himself, though repeatedly deceived by the treacherous attempts of your people like burning in the lac-house etc., and therefore fit to be killed by him with the help of the Supreme Person, nevertheless said, 'I will not fight.' He felt weak, overcome as he was by his love and extreme compassion for his relatives. He was also filled with fear, not knowing what was righteous and what unrighteous. His mind was tortured by grief, because of the thought of future separation from his relations. So he threw away his bow and arrow and sat on the chariot as if to fast to death.

verses.devanagari:
उत्सन्नकुलधर्माणां मनुष्याणां जनार्दन।
नरकेऽनियतं वासो भवतीत्यनुशुश्रुम ॥1.44॥
verses.transliteration:
1.44 utsannakuladharmāṇāṃ manuṣyāṇāṃ janārdana 
narake niyataṃ vāso bhavatīty anuśuśruma

translations.adidevananda: 1.44 For those whose clan-laws are destroyed, dwelling in hell is ordained, O Kṛṣṇa; thus have we heard.
translations.gambhirananda: 1.44 O Janārdana, we have heard that living in hell becomes inevitable for those persons whose family duties get destroyed.
translations.zaehner: (44) A sure abode in hell there is for men who bring to naught the firmly laws; so, Kṛṣṇa, have we heard.
translations.sivananda: 1.44. We have heard, O Janārdana, that inevitable is the dwelling for an unknown period in hell for those men in whose families the religious practices have been destroyed.
translations.gemini:
For men whose family laws (kuladharmāḥ) are destroyed, 
 O Agitator of men (Janārdana), 
 Dwelling in hell is inevitable; 
 Thus we have heard.
translations.stoller_miller:
Krishna, we have heard
that a place in hell
is reserved for men
who undermine family duties.

commentaries.ramanuja_sanskrit: ॥ 1.44 ॥ अर्जुन उवाच -- संजय उवाच -- स तु पार्थो महामनाः परमकारुणिको दीर्घबन्धुः परमधार्मिकः सभ्रातृको भवद्भिः अतिघोरैः मारणैः जतुगृहादिभिः असकृद् वञ्चितः अपि परमपुरुषसहायः अपि हनिष्यमाणान् भवदीयान् विलोक्य बन्धुस्नेहेन परमया च कृपया धर्माधर्मभयेन च अतिमात्रस्विन्नसर्वगात्रः सर्वथा अहं न योत्स्यामि इति उक्त्वा बन्धुविश्लेषजनितशोकसंविग्नमानसः सशरं चापं विसृज्य रथोपस्थे उपाविशत् ।
commentaries.madhva_sanskrit: ॥ 1.44 ॥ Sri Madhvacharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.11.
commentaries.madhusudana_sanskrit: ॥ 1.44 ॥ बन्धुवधपर्यवसायी युद्धाध्यवसायोऽपि सर्वथा पापिष्ठतरः किं पुनर्युद्धमिति वक्तुं तदध्यवसायेनात्मानं शोचन्नाह -- यदीदृशी ते बुद्धिः कुतस्तर्हि युद्धभिनिवेशेनागतोऽसीति न वक्तव्यम्। अविमृश्यकारितया मयौद्धत्यस्य कृतत्वादिति भावः।
commentaries.sankara_english: 1.44 Sri Sankaracharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.10.
commentaries.ramanuja_english: 1.26 - 1.47 Arjuna said --- Sañjaya said -- Sañjaya continued: The high-minded Arjuna, extremely kind, deeply friendly, and supremely righteous, having brothers like himself, though repeatedly deceived by the treacherous attempts of your people like burning in the lac-house etc., and therefore fit to be killed by him with the help of the Supreme Person, nevertheless said, 'I will not fight.' He felt weak, overcome as he was by his love and extreme compassion for his relatives. He was also filled with fear, not knowing what was righteous and what unrighteous. His mind was tortured by grief, because of the thought of future separation from his relations. So he threw away his bow and arrow and sat on the chariot as if to fast to death.

verses.devanagari:
अहो बत महत्पापं कर्तुं व्यवसिता वयम्।
यद्राज्यसुखलोभेन हन्तुं स्वजनमुद्यताः ॥1.45॥
verses.transliteration:
1.45 aho bata mahat pāpaṃ kartuṃ vyavasitā vayam 
yad rājyasukhalobhena hantuṃ svajanam udyatāḥ

translations.adidevananda: 1.45 Alas! We have resolved to commit a great sin in that we are ready to slay our kith and kin out of desire for sovereignty and enjoyments.
translations.gambhirananda: 1.45 What a pity that we have resolved to commit a great sin by being eager to kill our own kith and kin, out of greed for the pleasures of a kingdom!
translations.zaehner: (45) Ah! Ah! so are we [really] bent on committing a monstrous evil deed? intent, as we are on slaughtering our own folk because we lust for the sweets of sovereignty.
translations.sivananda: 1.45. Alas! We are involved in a great sin, in that we are prepared to kill our kinsmen, through greed for the pleasures of a kingdom.
translations.gemini:
Alas! A great sin (pāpa), 
 We are resolved to commit, 
 Since, by the greed for the pleasures of a kingdom, 
 We are prepared to kill our own people.
translations.stoller_miller:
I lament the great sin
we commit when our greed
for kingship and pleasures
drives us to kill our kinsmen.

commentaries.ramanuja_sanskrit: ॥ 1.45 ॥ अर्जुन उवाच -- संजय उवाच -- स तु पार्थो महामनाः परमकारुणिको दीर्घबन्धुः परमधार्मिकः सभ्रातृको भवद्भिः अतिघोरैः मारणैः जतुगृहादिभिः असकृद् वञ्चितः अपि परमपुरुषसहायः अपि हनिष्यमाणान् भवदीयान् विलोक्य बन्धुस्नेहेन परमया च कृपया धर्माधर्मभयेन च अतिमात्रस्विन्नसर्वगात्रः सर्वथा अहं न योत्स्यामि इति उक्त्वा बन्धुविश्लेषजनितशोकसंविग्नमानसः सशरं चापं विसृज्य रथोपस्थे उपाविशत् ।
commentaries.madhva_sanskrit: ॥ 1.45 ॥ Sri Madhvacharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.11.
commentaries.madhusudana_sanskrit: ॥ 1.45 ॥ ननु तव वैराग्येऽपि भीमसेनादीनां युद्धोत्सुकत्वाद्बन्धुवधो भविष्यत्येव त्वया पुनः किं विधेयमित्यत आह -- प्राणादपि प्रकृष्टो धर्मः प्राणभृतामहिंसा पापानिष्पतेः तस्माज्जीवनापेक्षया मरणमेव मम क्षेमतरमत्यन्तं हितं भवेत्।'प्रियतरम्' इति पाठेऽपि सवार्थः। अप्रतीकारं स्वप्राणत्राणाय व्यापारमकुर्वाणं बन्धुवधाध्यवसायमात्रेणापि प्रायश्चित्तान्तरहितं वा। तथाच प्राणान्तप्रायश्चित्तेनैव शुद्धिर्भविष्यतीत्यर्थः।
commentaries.sankara_english: 1.45 Sri Sankaracharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.10.
commentaries.ramanuja_english: 1.26 - 1.47 Arjuna said --- Sañjaya said -- Sañjaya continued: The high-minded Arjuna, extremely kind, deeply friendly, and supremely righteous, having brothers like himself, though repeatedly deceived by the treacherous attempts of your people like burning in the lac-house etc., and therefore fit to be killed by him with the help of the Supreme Person, nevertheless said, 'I will not fight.' He felt weak, overcome as he was by his love and extreme compassion for his relatives. He was also filled with fear, not knowing what was righteous and what unrighteous. His mind was tortured by grief, because of the thought of future separation from his relations. So he threw away his bow and arrow and sat on the chariot as if to fast to death.

verses.devanagari:
यदि मामप्रतीकारमशस्त्रं शस्त्रपाणयः।
धार्तराष्ट्रा रणे हन्युस्तन्मे क्षेमतरं भवेत् ॥1.46॥
verses.transliteration:
1.46 yadi mām apratīkāram aśastraṃ śastrapāṇayaḥ 
dhārtarāṣṭrā raṇe hanyus tan me kṣemataraṃ bhavet

translations.adidevananda: 1.46 If the well-armed sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra should slay me in battle, unresisting and unarmed, that will be better for me.
translations.gambhirananda: 1.46 If, in this battle, the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra armed with weapons kill me who am non-resistant and unarmed, that will be more beneficial to me.
translations.zaehner: (40) O let the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, arms in hand, slay me in battle though I, unarmed myself, will offer no defense; therein were greater happiness for me.'
translations.sivananda: 1.46. If the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra with weapons in hand should slay me in battle, unresisting and unarmed, that would be better for me.
translations.gemini:
If, unresisting, 
 Unarmed, the weapons in their hands, 
 The sons of Dhritarashtra should kill me in battle, 
 That would be more peaceful (kṣematara) for me.
translations.stoller_miller:
If Dhritarashtra's armed sons
kill me in battle when I am unarmed
and offer no resistance,
it will be my reward.”

commentaries.ramanuja_sanskrit: ॥ 1.46 ॥ अर्जुन उवाच -- संजय उवाच -- स तु पार्थो महामनाः परमकारुणिको दीर्घबन्धुः परमधार्मिकः सभ्रातृको भवद्भिः अतिघोरैः मारणैः जतुगृहादिभिः असकृद् वञ्चितः अपि परमपुरुषसहायः अपि हनिष्यमाणान् भवदीयान् विलोक्य बन्धुस्नेहेन परमया च कृपया धर्माधर्मभयेन च अतिमात्रस्विन्नसर्वगात्रः सर्वथा अहं न योत्स्यामि इति उक्त्वा बन्धुविश्लेषजनितशोकसंविग्नमानसः सशरं चापं विसृज्य रथोपस्थे उपाविशत् ।
commentaries.madhva_sanskrit: ॥ 1.46 ॥ Sri Madhvacharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.11.
commentaries.madhusudana_sanskrit: ॥ 1.46 ॥ ततः किं वृत्तमित्यपेक्षायां संख्ये संग्रामे रथोपस्थे रथस्योपर्युपविशेश। पूर्वं युद्धार्थमवलोकनार्थं चोत्थितः सन् शोकेन संविग्नं पीडितं मानसं यस्य सः। इति श्रीमत्परमहंसपरिद्वμककज्ककदृकच्ककर्यविश्वेश्वरसरस्वतीपादशिष्यसूनुमधुसूदनसरस्वतीविरचितायां श्रीमद्भगवद्गीतागूढार्थदीपिकायां प्रथमोऽध्यायः ॥ 1 ॥
commentaries.sankara_english: 1.46 Sri Sankaracharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.10.
commentaries.ramanuja_english: 1.26 - 1.47 Arjuna said --- Sañjaya said -- Sañjaya continued: The high-minded Arjuna, extremely kind, deeply friendly, and supremely righteous, having brothers like himself, though repeatedly deceived by the treacherous attempts of your people like burning in the lac-house etc., and therefore fit to be killed by him with the help of the Supreme Person, nevertheless said, 'I will not fight.' He felt weak, overcome as he was by his love and extreme compassion for his relatives. He was also filled with fear, not knowing what was righteous and what unrighteous. His mind was tortured by grief, because of the thought of future separation from his relations. So he threw away his bow and arrow and sat on the chariot as if to fast to death.

verses.devanagari:
सञ्जय उवाच
एवमुक्त्वाऽर्जुनः संख्ये रथोपस्थ उपाविशत्।
विसृज्य सशरं चापं शोकसंविग्नमानसः ॥1.47॥
verses.transliteration:
1.47 evam uktvārjunaḥ saṃkhye rathopastha upāviśat 
visṛjya saśaraṃ cāpaṃ śokasaṃvignamānasaḥ

translations.adidevananda: 1.47 Sañjaya said -- Having spoken thus on the battle-field, Arjuna threw aside his bow and arrows and sat down on the seat of the chariot, his heart overwhelmed with grief.
translations.gambhirananda: 1.47 Sañjaya narrated: Having said so, Arjuna, with a mind afflicted with sorrow, sat down on the chariot in the midst of the battle, casting aside the bow along with the arrows.
translations.zaehner: (47) So saying Arjuna sat down upon the chariot-scat [though] battle [hart begun], let slip his bow and arrows, his mind distraught with grief.
translations.sivananda: 1.47. Sañjaya said -- Having thus spoken in the midst of the battlefield, Arjuna, casting away his bow and arrow, sat down on the seat of the chariot with his mind overwhelmed with sorrow.
translations.gemini:
Sañjaya said: 
 Having spoken thus in the battle, Arjuna 
 Sat down on the seat of the chariot, 
 Casting aside his bow along with the arrows, 
 His mind overwhelmed by sorrow.
translations.stoller_miller:
Saying this in the time of war,
Arjuna slumped into the chariot
and laid down his bow and arrows,
his mind tormented by grief.

commentaries.ramanuja_sanskrit: ॥ 1.47 ॥ अर्जुन उवाच -- संजय उवाच -- स तु पार्थो महामनाः परमकारुणिको दीर्घबन्धुः परमधार्मिकः सभ्रातृको भवद्भिः अतिघोरैः मारणैः जतुगृहादिभिः असकृद् वञ्चितः अपि परमपुरुषसहायः अपि हनिष्यमाणान् भवदीयान् विलोक्य बन्धुस्नेहेन परमया च कृपया धर्माधर्मभयेन च अतिमात्रस्विन्नसर्वगात्रः सर्वथा अहं न योत्स्यामि इति उक्त्वा बन्धुविश्लेषजनितशोकसंविग्नमानसः सशरं चापं विसृज्य रथोपस्थे उपाविशत् ।
commentaries.madhva_sanskrit: ॥ 1.47 ॥ Sri Madhvacharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.11.
commentaries.madhusudana_sanskrit: ॥ 1.47 ॥ च्द्धत् ग्ठ्ठड्डण्ध्ठ्ठड़ण्ठ्ठद्धन्र्ठ्ठ ड्डत्ड्ड ददृद्य ड़दृथ्र्थ्र्ड्ढदद्य दृद द्यण्त्द्म द्मथ्दृत्त्ठ्ठ. च्र्ण्ड्ढ ड़दृथ्र्थ्र्ड्ढदद्यठ्ठद्धन्र् द्मद्यठ्ठद्धद्यद्म ढद्धदृथ्र् 2.11.  एक्तॠक्रॠज्ॠक़्क्रक्ष्च्र्ॠ च्ठ्ठदद्मत्त्द्धत्द्य क्दृथ्र्थ्र्ड्ढदद्यठ्ठद्धन्र् - च्द्धत् ग्ठ्ठड्डण्द्वद्मद्वड्डठ्ठद च्ठ्ठद्धठ्ठद्मध्र्ठ्ठद्यत् क्ण्ठ्ठद्रद्यड्ढद्ध 2 क़्ज्
commentaries.sankara_english: 1.47 Sri Sankaracharya did not comment on this sloka. The commentary starts from 2.10.
